segments.text: This new Adobe killer has arrived. There
are three reasons why this app is going
to replace Adobe Creative Cloud. First,
Adobe makes you switch apps. Illustrator
for vectors, Photoshop for pictures, and
in design for layouts, but Affinity put
all three in one app, and you can
instantly switch without even breaking
your flow. That's easy, but what about
speed? Adobe is heavy and slow. Whenever
you are opening a big file, it lags.
Whenever you zoom a lot, it lags.
[music]
But Affinity, it is made for zero lag.
It's light and it handles huge files
[music] right away. They claim that the
response rate is very good. But here is
the real reason why Adobe is scared. The
price. Adobe hits you with $70 every
single month. It's endless payment.
While Affinity Designer, it's free.
Totally free. And you pay nothing
